content.problemStatement:
Write a program to display all possible **continuous parts** of a given array. A continuous part of an array is a group of numbers that are next to each other in the array, without skipping any numbers.

Your task is to find and print every such group from the array.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer, n —,~n~ —, n — the size of the array.
The second line contains nnn space-separated integers representing the elements of the array.


Output Format
Print all the continuous subarrays of the given array in **lexicographical order**. Each subarray should be printed on a new line, and the elements of the subarray should be separated by spaces.
constraints: −103≤N≤103-10^3 \leq N \leq 10^3−103≤N≤103 (((where a[i]a[i]a[i] is an element of the array)))
